page: Amazon Web Services m7gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processors utilizing the ARM64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 24495.36 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12993.0. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a Passmark memory score rated as strong and a large-block memory write bandwidth of 100.5 GB/sec. However, cryptographic performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Passmark CPU encryption test score of 2224.38, which is likely due to the lack of specialized instruction sets like AVX or AMX, though basic AES cryptography is supported.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high resource density and predictable performance without hyperthreading overhead. The inclusion of 950 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage eliminates the need for external block storage for high-IOPS tasks, making it highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, and redis-based caching. It is also well-suited for general-purpose application hosting, software compilation, and light language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by 16 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory, 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers balanced compute and memory performance, highlighted by strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 100.5 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score in the top tier. While cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck due to the absence of AVX and AMX instruction sets, its dedicated core allocation and integrated high-speed local storage provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads. The instance is highly suited for database operations, static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 950 GB local NVMe SSD storage for database and web workloads.

tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong memory write bandwidth.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1
64.0 GB of system memory paired with 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong memory write bandwidth of 100.5 GB/sec for data-intensive tasks
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and Redis caching
